summary: lineitem pace flags truncation (truncated + truncation_note fields and a stderr warning) when an order has more line items than --limit, since it fetches a single page; since counts 'scanned' after the network-prefix filter so multi-network mirror rows do not inflate it; gamCacheItems documents its network-unqualified key as a known, correctness-safe limitation.
reason: Non-critical quality items raised in library review round 3 (PR #1243): the schedule-pace ranking could silently omit lower-ranked line items, the scanned count over-reported for multi-network mirrors, and the write-side cache-key collision across networks was undocumented (left as-is because re-keying would change the id surfaced by 'since').
validated_outcome: go build + full cli test suite pass in both trees; gofmt clean; lineitem pace emits truncated=true with a note when nextPageToken is present or the page fills.
